news.title: Oil prices drop and stocks enjoy cautious rally on US-Iran peace progress
news.summary: There is cautious optimism on financial markets that a US-Iran peace deal will ease the hit to the global economy caused by their war.  Confirmation late on Sunday that an agreement had been reached to end the conflict of three-and-a-half months saw Brent crude oil prices tumble at the start of trading in Asia, building on declines seen last week.  The international benchmark tumbled by more than 4% to $83 a barrel - just $11 higher than the level seen before the US-Israeli attacks on Iran began at the end of February.
